zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Scudamore, Knight於1375年出生在英國賀福爾肯丘其時，他的父親Sir Jenkin Scudamore41歲，母親Elizabeth ferch Owain Glendower5歲。他和Alys ferch Owain Glyndwr至少有6個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1434年在他的家鄉享年59歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Scudamore,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Skidmore
meanings: English (Staffordshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Scudamore, which most probably arises from a Norman French nickname for a knight who used a particular type of leather shield, from the unrecorded Norman French phrase, escu de more ‘Moor's shield’, a reference to a type of Moorish leather shield called an adarga, which was in widespread use not only in medieval Iberia and north Africa but also in Brittany.

History: The manor of Upton Scudamore in Wiltshire was held by Peter de Skydemore in 1216. The same manor was held by Godfrey Escudamore shortly after 1150. The Scudamore family claim descent from Ralph, recorded in Domesday Book (1086) as holder of Opeton (Upton), whose son was known as Reginald Escudemor.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Scudamore, Knight was born in 1375, in Kentchurch, Herefordshire, England, his father, Sir Jenkin Scudamore, was 41 and his mother, Elizabeth ferch Owain Glendower, was 5. He had at least 6 sons and 4 daughters with Alys ferch Owain Glyndwr. He died in 1434, in his hometown, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John de Scudamore, Knight est né en 1375, à Kentchurch, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Jenkin Scudamore, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth ferch Owain Glendower, avait 5 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles avec Alys ferch Owain Glyndwr. Il est décédé en 1434, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 59 ans.

Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie con Alys ferch Owain Glyndwr. Morì nel 1434, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 59 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Scudamore, Knight(이)가 1375년 영국 허퍼드실르 켄트c후르치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Jenkin Scudamore(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth ferch Owain Glendower(은)는 5세였다. 그는 Alys ferch Owain Glyndwr(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1434년 그의 고향에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Scudamore, Knight родился в 1375 г. в местности кэнчурч, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Jenkin Scudamore было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth ferch Owain Glendower, 5. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери от Alys ferch Owain Glyndwr. Он умер в 1434 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 59 лет.
